title: A Search for Infrared Laser Emission with the Hobby-Eberly Telescope
author: Fan, Pinchen
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The search for extraterrestrial intelligence (SETI) has traditionally been done at optical or radio wavelengths. We are undertaking a novel commensal SETI search for laser technosignatures utilizing near-infrared observations of more than 50 nearby M-dwarfs over a period of 3 years with the Habitable Zone Planet Finder (HPF) on the 10- meter Hobby-Eberly Telescope. These high-resolution fiber-fed spectra taken for planet detection with the radial velocity method enable us to search for spectrally unresolved laser emission that outshines the host star. We present an analysis pipeline under development targeting the wavelength region from 8100 Å to 12800 Å, discussing how we account for contamination from sky absorption and emission features which could mimic such signals. We discuss possible future steps such as placing upper limits on the presence of unresolved laser emission in these spectra.
